textByHashes.9742c1d494e90853cfb84b662c00e47be9a19ae5: Hello! You must be the adventurer that helped us get out of Menaphos. Thank you.
textByHashes.d8bcb21fbf3aa81ede67d6244f7db745bbf67cb3: Not great. I'd just gone over there to purchase some goods from the market. When I got back to the gates, they'd been sealed shut.
textByHashes.9876aed139b5a52812f59ba539b98bb320376b95: They were still allowing citizens from Sophanem to return at that point, but I foolishly chose to stay. I was scared of catching the pox.
textByHashes.1181a22de61d8b8300cb312392655a6e790a674e: By the time I'd changed my mind, they'd stopped letting anyone through. Thankfully, I had some friends there with a spare bed.
textByHashes.d60a0e130a41550c61b9644b2482e3651f7c9811: No. Many didn't have anywhere to go. Violence broke out in some parts of the city.
textByHashes.1afbdfe3dc8d62f38160fc74273ade16d9d6452b: They quickly put a stop to it in the Imperial and Merchant quarters, but there was still some unrest in the Workers district when I was finally able to leave.
textByHashes.2567749fd569472f25e461416b4349e8b8370866: They were brutal. They even shut off the port from the rest of the city over fears of the plague getting in that way. Many of their own citizens are still trapped there.
textByHashes.012255420b263d7fdfa2b8ce0e5ec54c19774515: Anyway, let's not talk any more of this. Is there anything else I can do for you.
textByHashes.31126e5ae69ec512789a6c6e25b32e99570cce9c: Hello again! It's so nice to be out of Menaphos. Once again, thank you.
